aberration.en: something that is unusual or unexpected and different from what is normal

ex.en: The scandal was described as an aberration in an otherwise spotless career.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들은 흠잡을 데 없던 경력에서의 일탈로 묘사되었다.

aberration.en: a temporary disturbance or abnormality in someone's mental state

ex.en: He blamed his outburst on a momentary mental aberration.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 격한 행동을 일시적인 정신적 착란 탓으로 돌렸다.

aberration.en: a fault in a lens or mirror that causes a blurred or distorted image

ex.en: Cheap camera lenses often suffer from chromatic aberration.
ex.ko: 값싼 카메라 렌즈는 흔히 색수차를 겪는다.

abhor.en: feel strong hatred or disgust toward something

ex.en: She abhors any form of cruelty to animals.
ex.ko: 그녀는 동물에 대한 어떤 형태의 잔인함도 혐오한다.

abhorrent.en: causing strong feelings of disgust or hatred

ex.en: Torture is abhorrent to most civilized societies.
ex.ko: 고문은 대부분의 문명 사회에서 혐오스러운 것으로 여겨진다.

abhorrently.en: in a way that causes strong disgust or hatred

ex.en: The prisoners were treated abhorrently by their captors.
ex.ko: 포로들은 자신들을 붙잡은 자들에게 혐오스러운 대우를 받았다.

abject.en: extremely bad, unpleasant, and without any hope or dignity

ex.en: They were living in abject poverty.
ex.ko: 그들은 극도로 비참한 가난 속에 살고 있었다.

abject.en: showing a complete lack of pride or dignity, especially through fear or shame

ex.en: He offered an abject apology for his mistake.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 실수에 대해 비굴할 정도로 사과했다.

ex.en: Wildflowers grow abundantly in this valley.
ex.ko: 이 골짜기에는 야생화가 풍부하게 자란다.

abundantly.en: used to emphasize that something is extremely obvious or clear

ex.en: She made it abundantly clear that she disagreed.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신이 동의하지 않는다는 것을 매우 분명하게 밝혔다.

accountability.en: the fact of being responsible for your actions and willing to explain them

ex.en: The new policy increases accountability among managers.
ex.ko: 새 정책은 관리자들 사이의 책무성을 높인다.

accrue.en: gradually increase in amount over time

ex.en: Interest accrues on the account monthly.
ex.ko: 그 계좌에는 매달 이자가 누적된다.

accrue.en: come to someone as a natural result or right

ex.en: Great benefits accrue to anyone who invests early.
ex.ko: 일찍 투자하는 사람에게는 큰 이익이 돌아간다.

acreage.en: the area of a piece of land, especially measured in acres

ex.en: The company purchased additional acreage for the new factory.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 새 공장을 위해 추가 부지를 매입했다.

acrobatic.en: involving skillful and energetic body movements, like those of an acrobat

ex.en: The goalkeeper made an acrobatic save.
ex.ko: 그 골키퍼는 곡예 같은 선방을 펼쳤다.

acrobatically.en: in a way that involves skillful and energetic body movements

ex.en: She leapt acrobatically across the stage.
ex.ko: 그녀는 무대를 가로질러 곡예하듯 뛰어올랐다.

acrobatics.en: difficult physical actions involving skillful control of the body

ex.en: The circus performers amazed the crowd with their acrobatics.
ex.ko: 서커스 공연자들은 곡예로 관중을 놀라게 했다.

acrobatics.en: impressively skillful or complicated performance, often used figuratively

ex.en: The lawyer's verbal acrobatics confused the jury.
ex.ko: 그 변호사의 말재간 같은 곡예는 배심원단을 혼란스럽게 했다.

ad lib.en: done or said without any preparation or advance planning

ex.en: He gave an ad lib speech when the microphone failed.
ex.ko: 마이크가 고장 나자 그는 즉흥 연설을 했다.

ad-lib.en: say or do something without preparing it in advance

ex.en: The actor ad-libbed a joke when he forgot his line.
ex.ko: 그 배우는 대사를 잊어버리자 즉흥적으로 농담을 했다.

ad-lib.en: something said or done spontaneously without being planned

ex.en: Her ad-libs got more laughs than the script.
ex.ko: 그녀의 애드리브가 대본보다 더 많은 웃음을 자아냈다.

additive.en: a substance added to something, especially food, in small amounts for a particular purpose

ex.en: This bread contains no artificial additives.
ex.ko: 이 빵에는 인공 첨가물이 들어 있지 않다.

additive.en: produced by adding one thing to another

ex.en: The drug has an additive effect when combined with alcohol.
ex.ko: 그 약은 알코올과 함께 복용하면 상승 작용을 한다.

adept.en: very skilled or good at doing something

ex.en: She is adept at solving complex problems.
ex.ko: 그녀는 복잡한 문제를 해결하는 데 능숙하다.

adept.en: someone who is highly skilled in a particular field

ex.en: He is a true adept in the art of negotiation.
ex.ko: 그는 협상의 기술에 있어 진정한 달인이다.

adhere.en: act exactly according to a rule, plan, or set of beliefs

ex.en: All employees must adhere to the safety guidelines.
ex.ko: 모든 직원은 안전 수칙을 준수해야 한다.

ex.en: The stamp wouldn't adhere to the envelope.
ex.ko: 그 우표는 봉투에 잘 붙지 않았다.

adhesion.en: the property of sticking firmly to a surface or substance

ex.en: This glue provides strong adhesion to wood and metal.
ex.ko: 이 접착제는 나무와 금속에 강하게 접착된다.

adhesion.cue: 수술 후 장기끼리 비정상적으로 붙는 증상
adhesion.en: a band of scar tissue that abnormally joins body parts together, often after surgery

ex.en: The patient developed adhesions after the operation.
ex.ko: 그 환자는 수술 후 유착이 생겼다.

adornment.en: something added to make a person or thing more attractive; the act of decorating

ex.en: The dress was simple, with little adornment.
ex.ko: 그 드레스는 장식이 거의 없이 단순했다.

adulterate.en: make something impure or of lower quality by adding another substance

ex.en: The company was accused of adulterating its olive oil with cheaper oils.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 값싼 기름으로 올리브유에 불순물을 섞었다는 혐의를 받았다.

affectation.en: behavior, speech, or an expression that is not natural or genuine, done to impress others

ex.en: His British accent was just an affectation.
ex.ko: 그의 영국식 억양은 그저 가식이었다.

ex.en: She was aghast at the mess in the kitchen.
ex.ko: 그녀는 부엌의 엉망진창인 모습에 경악했다.

agile.en: able to move quickly and easily

ex.en: The dancer's agile movements amazed the audience.
ex.ko: 그 무용수의 민첩한 동작은 관객을 놀라게 했다.

agile.en: able to think quickly and clearly

ex.en: You need an agile mind to solve these puzzles.
ex.ko: 이 퍼즐을 풀려면 기민한 두뇌가 필요하다.

agility.en: the ability to move quickly and easily

ex.en: Gymnasts need great agility and balance.
ex.ko: 체조 선수는 뛰어난 민첩성과 균형 감각이 필요하다.

all-encompassing.en: including everything; very broad in range

ex.en: The report offers an all-encompassing view of the industry.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 업계에 대한 포괄적인 시각을 제공한다.

allegorical.en: using symbols or fictional characters to represent a deeper moral or political meaning

ex.en: The novel is an allegorical critique of totalitarianism.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 전체주의에 대한 우화적 비판이다.

allegory.cue: 동물 등을 등장시켜 교훈을 전하는 짧은 이야기
allegory.en: a story, poem, or picture that can be understood to have a hidden moral or political meaning

ex.en: Animal Farm is often read as an allegory of the Russian Revolution.
ex.ko: 동물농장은 흔히 러시아 혁명에 대한 우화로 읽힌다.

allegory.en: a style of writing or art that uses symbols to represent abstract ideas

ex.en: The painting uses allegory to depict the passage of time.
ex.ko: 그 그림은 풍유법을 사용해 시간의 흐름을 묘사한다.

allot.en: give a share of something to someone as part of a plan

ex.en: Each speaker was allotted ten minutes.
ex.ko: 각 발표자에게 10분씩이 할당되었다.

allotment.en: a share of something that has been given to a particular person or purpose

ex.en: Each department received its allotment of the budget.
ex.ko: 각 부서는 예산 중 자신의 할당량을 받았다.

allude.en: mention something in an indirect way

ex.en: She alluded to problems in the company without giving details.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자세히 설명하지 않고 회사의 문제들을 넌지시 언급했다.

allusion.en: an indirect reference to something, without mentioning it directly

ex.en: The poem is full of allusions to Greek mythology.
ex.ko: 그 시는 그리스 신화에 대한 암시로 가득하다.

amorphous.en: having no clear shape or form

ex.en: The clay was still an amorphous lump.
ex.ko: 그 점토는 아직 형태가 없는 덩어리였다.

amorphous.en: lacking clear organization, structure, or focus

ex.en: The protest movement remained amorphous, without any clear leadership.
ex.ko: 그 시위 운동은 뚜렷한 지도부 없이 체계가 없는 채로 남아 있었다.

amorphously.en: in a way that lacks clear shape, form, or organization

ex.en: The group was amorphously organized, with no fixed roles.
ex.ko: 그 모임은 고정된 역할 없이 형태 없이 조직되어 있었다.

anarchic.en: lacking any order, control, or authority

ex.en: The city fell into an anarchic state after the government collapsed.
ex.ko: 정부가 붕괴된 후 그 도시는 무정부 상태에 빠졌다.

angst.en: a strong feeling of anxiety or worry, often about life in general

ex.en: Teenage angst is a common theme in coming-of-age films.
ex.ko: 청소년기의 고뇌는 성장 영화의 흔한 주제다.

angsty.en: full of anxiety or emotional distress, often in a dramatic or exaggerated way

ex.en: The band is known for its angsty lyrics about heartbreak.
ex.ko: 그 밴드는 실연에 대한 고뇌에 찬 가사로 유명하다.

angular.en: having sharp corners or angles rather than curves

ex.en: The building has a striking angular design.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 인상적인 각진 디자인을 가지고 있다.

angular.en: (of a person) thin, with bones that are clearly visible

ex.en: He had a tall, angular frame.
ex.ko: 그는 키가 크고 뼈가 앙상한 체격이었다.

angularity.en: the quality of having sharp corners or angles

ex.en: The sculpture is defined by its harsh angularity.
ex.ko: 그 조각상은 날카로운 각짐이 특징이다.

annex.en: a building added to a larger main building

ex.en: The library annex houses rare manuscripts.
ex.ko: 그 도서관 별관에는 희귀 필사본들이 보관되어 있다.

annex.en: take control of a country or region and make it part of your own, usually by force

ex.en: The empire annexed the neighboring territory after the war.
ex.ko: 그 제국은 전쟁 후 이웃 영토를 합병했다.

anonymity.en: the state of not being known by name or identity

ex.en: The internet allows a certain degree of anonymity.
ex.ko: 인터넷은 어느 정도의 익명성을 허용한다.

antediluvian.en: extremely old-fashioned or outdated, as if belonging to an ancient era

ex.en: His antediluvian views on marriage shocked the younger staff.
ex.ko: 결혼에 대한 그의 구시대적인 견해는 젊은 직원들을 놀라게 했다.

anthology.cue: 여러 작가의 글을 모아 엮은 책
anthology.en: a collection of pieces of writing, such as poems or stories, published together in one book

ex.en: The teacher assigned an anthology of short stories.
ex.ko: 선생님은 단편 소설 선집을 과제로 내주었다.

anthropological.en: relating to the scientific study of humans, their societies, and their cultures

ex.en: She conducted anthropological research in a remote village.
ex.ko: 그녀는 외딴 마을에서 인류학적 연구를 수행했다.

anthropologically.en: from the perspective of the scientific study of humans and their cultures

ex.en: Anthropologically speaking, the ritual reflects the community's values.
ex.ko: 인류학적으로 말하자면, 그 의식은 공동체의 가치관을 반영한다.

anticlimactically.en: in a way that ends disappointingly after building up expectation

ex.en: The long-awaited match ended anticlimactically in a scoreless draw.
ex.ko: 오래 기다린 그 경기는 무득점 무승부로 김빠지게 끝났다.

antidote.en: a substance that stops or controls the effects of a poison

ex.en: Doctors quickly gave him the antidote for the snake venom.
ex.ko: 의사들은 그에게 뱀독에 대한 해독제를 신속히 투여했다.

antidote.en: something that helps to reduce or counteract an unpleasant situation or feeling

ex.en: Regular exercise can be a good antidote to stress.
ex.ko: 규칙적인 운동은 스트레스에 대한 좋은 해결책이 될 수 있다.

ex.en: Her calm attitude was the antithesis of his nervous energy.
ex.ko: 그녀의 차분한 태도는 그의 초조한 에너지와 정반대였다.

antithetical.en: directly opposed or contrasted in nature or purpose

ex.en: His methods are antithetical to traditional teaching.
ex.ko: 그의 방법은 전통적인 교육법과 정반대이다.

apostle.cue: 예수의 열두 제자 중 한 명
apostle.en: one of the twelve original followers of Jesus Christ chosen to spread his teachings

ex.en: The church was named after the apostle Paul.
ex.ko: 그 교회는 사도 바울의 이름을 따서 지어졌다.

apostle.en: a strong and enthusiastic supporter or promoter of a cause or idea

ex.en: She became a leading apostle of environmental reform.
ex.ko: 그녀는 환경 개혁의 대표적인 전도사가 되었다.

apprehend.en: understand or grasp the meaning of something

ex.en: It took him a while to apprehend the full scope of the problem.
ex.ko: 그는 문제의 전체 규모를 파악하는 데 시간이 걸렸다.

ex.en: The suspect was apprehended at the airport.
ex.ko: 용의자는 공항에서 체포되었다.

aptitude.en: a natural ability to do something or learn something well

ex.en: She shows a real aptitude for mathematics.
ex.ko: 그녀는 수학에 진짜 소질을 보인다.

arcane.en: understood by very few people; mysterious or secret

ex.en: The contract was full of arcane legal terms.
ex.ko: 그 계약서는 난해한 법률 용어로 가득했다.

arcanely.en: in a way that is mysterious and understood by very few people

ex.en: The rules were arcanely written and hard to follow.
ex.ko: 그 규정은 난해하게 쓰여 있어 따르기 어려웠다.

archetypal.en: representing the most typical example of a particular kind of thing

ex.en: He is the archetypal absent-minded professor.
ex.ko: 그는 전형적인 건망증 심한 교수다.

archetype.en: a very typical example of a certain type of person or thing

ex.en: The film's hero is an archetype of the reluctant leader.
ex.ko: 그 영화의 주인공은 마지못해 이끄는 지도자의 전형이다.

archival.en: relating to historical documents or records that are kept and preserved

ex.en: The museum keeps archival footage from the 1950s.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 1950년대의 기록 영상을 보관하고 있다.

ardor.en: a feeling of great enthusiasm or passion for something

ex.en: She pursued her research with great ardor.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대단한 열정으로 연구에 매진했다.

arduous.en: requiring a lot of effort and often taking a long time; very difficult

ex.en: The climbers faced an arduous journey to the summit.
ex.ko: 등반가들은 정상까지 고된 여정을 마주했다.

arduously.en: in a way that requires a lot of effort and difficulty

ex.en: They worked arduously for months to finish the project.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 프로젝트를 끝내기 위해 몇 달 동안 고되게 일했다.

armory.en: a place where weapons are stored, or sometimes made

ex.en: The soldiers collected their rifles from the armory.
ex.ko: 병사들은 무기고에서 소총을 지급받았다.

armory.cue: 어떤 목적을 위해 갖춘 자원 전체
armory.en: the whole collection of resources or tools available for a particular purpose

ex.en: Persuasion is one of the most powerful tools in a lawyer's armory.
ex.ko: 설득력은 변호사가 갖춘 수단 중 가장 강력한 것 중 하나다.

articulation.en: the way in which someone pronounces words clearly

ex.en: The actor was praised for his clear articulation.
ex.ko: 그 배우는 명확한 발음으로 칭찬받았다.

articulation.en: the clear expression of an idea or feeling in words

ex.en: The report offers a careful articulation of the company's strategy.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 회사 전략에 대한 신중한 표현을 담고 있다.

artifact.en: an object made by a person, especially one of historical or cultural interest

ex.en: The museum displays artifacts from ancient Egypt.
ex.ko: 그 박물관은 고대 이집트의 유물을 전시하고 있다.

artifice.en: clever but often deceptive methods used to achieve something

ex.en: He won her trust through artifice rather than honesty.
ex.ko: 그는 정직함이 아니라 술책으로 그녀의 신뢰를 얻었다.

assimilate.en: take in and fully understand information or ideas

ex.en: It takes time to assimilate so much new material.
ex.ko: 그렇게 많은 새 자료를 흡수하는 데는 시간이 걸린다.

assimilate.en: become or make someone become part of a different society, country, or group

ex.en: Many immigrants gradually assimilate into the local culture.
ex.ko: 많은 이민자들이 점차 현지 문화에 동화된다.

assimilation.en: the process of becoming part of a different society, country, or group

ex.en: The government promoted policies of cultural assimilation.
ex.ko: 정부는 문화적 동화 정책을 추진했다.

assimilation.en: the process of fully understanding and absorbing information or ideas

ex.en: Effective learning depends on the assimilation of new concepts.
ex.ko: 효과적인 학습은 새로운 개념의 흡수에 달려 있다.

astringent.en: having a sour or bitter taste that makes your mouth feel dry

ex.en: The wine had an astringent taste that lingered.
ex.ko: 그 와인은 입안에 오래 남는 떫은맛이 났다.

astringent.en: (of comments or criticism) sharp, harsh, and severe

ex.en: The critic wrote an astringent review of the film.
ex.ko: 그 평론가는 그 영화에 대해 신랄한 평을 썼다.

astringent.en: causing body tissue to contract, often used to describe a cosmetic or skin product

ex.en: She applied an astringent toner to close her pores.
ex.ko: 그녀는 모공을 조이기 위해 수렴 효과가 있는 토너를 발랐다.

athleticism.en: physical strength, fitness, and skill typical of an athlete

ex.en: The dancer's raw athleticism impressed the judges.
ex.ko: 그 무용수의 뛰어난 운동 능력은 심사위원들에게 깊은 인상을 남겼다.

attributive.en: (of an adjective) placed directly before the noun it describes, rather than after a verb

ex.en: In "the red car," "red" is used attributively.
ex.ko: 'the red car'에서 'red'는 한정적으로 쓰인 것이다.

attrition.en: a gradual reduction in the number of people or things over time

ex.en: The company reduced staff through natural attrition rather than layoffs.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 해고 대신 자연 감소를 통해 인력을 줄였다.

attrition.en: a long, gradual process of wearing down an opponent's strength or resistance

ex.en: The war became a bloody war of attrition.
ex.ko: 그 전쟁은 처참한 소모전이 되었다.

attuned.en: aware of and able to respond sensitively to something

ex.en: Good teachers are attuned to their students' needs.
ex.ko: 좋은 교사는 학생들의 요구에 민감하다.

austere.en: very plain and simple, without comfort or luxury

ex.en: The monks lived an austere life with few possessions.
ex.ko: 수도승들은 소지품이 거의 없는 소박한 삶을 살았다.

austere.en: stern and serious in manner or appearance

ex.en: The headmaster had an austere expression that intimidated students.
ex.ko: 교장은 학생들을 위축시키는 근엄한 표정을 가지고 있었다.

austerely.en: in a plain, simple manner without comfort or luxury

ex.en: The room was furnished austerely, with just a bed and a chair.
ex.ko: 그 방은 침대와 의자만 있는 검소한 가구로 꾸며져 있었다.

austerity.en: a government policy of reducing public spending, especially to reduce debt

ex.en: The country introduced austerity measures to cut its debt.
ex.ko: 그 나라는 부채를 줄이기 위해 긴축 정책을 도입했다.

austerity.en: a simple and plain way of living without luxury

ex.en: He lived a life of austerity even after becoming wealthy.
ex.ko: 그는 부자가 된 이후에도 검소한 삶을 살았다.

authenticity.en: the quality of being real, genuine, or true

ex.en: Experts questioned the authenticity of the painting.
ex.ko: 전문가들은 그 그림의 진위를 의심했다.

automate.en: make a process or system operate automatically, using machines or computers instead of people

ex.en: The factory automated most of its production line.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 생산 라인 대부분을 자동화했다.

autonomous.en: able to govern itself or act independently, without outside control

ex.en: The region became an autonomous province within the country.
ex.ko: 그 지역은 그 나라 안에서 자치주가 되었다.

autonomous.cue: 사람의 개입 없이 스스로 작동하는 기계
autonomous.en: (of a machine or vehicle) able to operate without human control

ex.en: The company is testing autonomous delivery drones.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 자율 배송 드론을 시험하고 있다.

autonomously.en: in a way that does not depend on outside control or help

ex.en: The robot can navigate the warehouse autonomously.
ex.ko: 그 로봇은 창고 안을 자율적으로 이동할 수 있다.

autopilot.en: a device that automatically keeps a plane, ship, or spacecraft on a steady course

ex.en: The pilot switched on the autopilot during the long flight.
ex.ko: 조종사는 긴 비행 동안 자동 조종 장치를 켰다.

autopilot.en: a state in which you act without conscious thought, out of habit

ex.en: After years at the job, she answers phones on autopilot.
ex.ko: 몇 년간 그 일을 해오면서 그녀는 거의 무의식적으로 전화를 받는다.

averse.en: strongly disliking or opposed to something

ex.en: He is not averse to taking risks in business.
ex.ko: 그는 사업에서 위험을 감수하는 것을 꺼리지 않는다.

ex.en: The outbreak was caused by an avian flu virus.
ex.ko: 그 발병은 조류 독감 바이러스 때문이었다.

axiom.en: a statement or rule that is widely accepted as true without needing proof

ex.en: "Honesty is the best policy" is treated almost as an axiom.
ex.ko: '정직이 최선의 방책이다'는 거의 자명한 이치로 여겨진다.

axiomatic.en: so obviously true that it does not need to be proved

ex.en: It is axiomatic that practice improves performance.
ex.ko: 연습이 실력을 향상시킨다는 것은 자명한 사실이다.

babble.en: make continuous meaningless sounds, like a baby

ex.en: The baby babbled happily in her crib.
ex.ko: 아기는 아기침대에서 즐겁게 옹알거렸다.

babble.en: talk quickly and continuously in a confused or excited way

ex.en: She babbled on about her trip for an hour.
ex.ko: 그녀는 한 시간 동안 자신의 여행에 대해 재잘거렸다.

banish.en: officially force someone to leave a country or place as punishment

ex.en: The king banished the traitor from the kingdom.
ex.ko: 왕은 반역자를 왕국에서 추방했다.

banish.en: get rid of an unpleasant feeling or thought completely

ex.en: She tried to banish her fears before the interview.
ex.ko: 그녀는 면접 전에 두려움을 떨쳐내려 애썼다.

banishment.en: the act of being officially forced to leave a country or place

ex.en: He was sentenced to banishment from the city.
ex.ko: 그는 도시에서 추방형을 선고받았다.

baroque.en: relating to the highly decorated style of European art, architecture, and music from the 17th and 18th centuries

ex.en: The cathedral is famous for its baroque architecture.
ex.ko: 그 대성당은 바로크 건축양식으로 유명하다.

baroque.en: extremely elaborate or complicated in style or design

ex.en: The novel's plot is almost baroque in its complexity.
ex.ko: 그 소설의 줄거리는 복잡함이 거의 바로크풍이다.

bauble.en: a cheap, showy piece of jewelry or decoration

ex.en: The market stall was full of colorful baubles.
ex.ko: 그 시장 좌판에는 화려한 색깔의 값싼 장신구들이 가득했다.

behest.en: an official or authoritative order or request

ex.en: She resigned at the behest of the board.
ex.ko: 그녀는 이사회의 지시에 따라 사임했다.

belligerence.en: an aggressive or hostile attitude, showing readiness to fight or argue

ex.en: His belligerence made negotiations difficult.
ex.ko: 그의 호전성 때문에 협상이 어려워졌다.

belligerent.en: hostile and aggressive, ready to fight or argue

ex.en: The customer became belligerent when his refund was denied.
ex.ko: 고객은 환불이 거절되자 적대적으로 변했다.

belligerent.en: a country or group that is engaged in a war or conflict

ex.en: The treaty required all belligerents to withdraw their forces.
ex.ko: 그 조약은 모든 교전국에 병력 철수를 요구했다.

beneficiary.en: a person who receives money, benefits, or advantages from something

ex.en: She is the sole beneficiary of her grandmother's will.
ex.ko: 그녀는 할머니 유언장의 유일한 수익자다.

bereft.en: sad and lonely because someone or something important has been lost

ex.en: She felt utterly bereft after her husband's death.
ex.ko: 그녀는 남편의 죽음 이후 완전히 상실감에 빠졌다.

bereft.cue: 당연히 있어야 할 것이 전혀 없는
bereft.en: completely lacking something that should normally be present

ex.en: The abandoned house was bereft of any furniture.
ex.ko: 그 버려진 집에는 가구가 전혀 없었다.

bereftly.en: in a way that shows sadness from loss or lack of something

ex.en: He wandered bereftly through the empty house.
ex.ko: 그는 텅 빈 집을 상실감에 젖은 채 헤매고 다녔다.

besiege.en: surround a place with armed forces in order to capture it

ex.en: Enemy troops besieged the castle for months.
ex.ko: 적군은 몇 달 동안 성을 포위했다.

besiege.en: overwhelm someone with a large number of requests, questions, or demands

ex.en: Reporters besieged the celebrity with questions.
ex.ko: 기자들은 그 유명인에게 질문 세례를 퍼부었다.

blandishment.en: flattering words or actions used to persuade someone to do something

ex.en: He was not swayed by the salesman's blandishments.
ex.ko: 그는 그 영업사원의 감언에 넘어가지 않았다.

blight.en: a plant disease that causes leaves and stems to wither and die

ex.en: A blight destroyed much of the potato crop.
ex.ko: 병충해가 감자 농작물 대부분을 망쳤다.

blight.en: something that damages or spoils an area, situation, or someone's life

ex.en: Unemployment remains a blight on the local economy.
ex.ko: 실업은 여전히 지역 경제의 골칫거리다.

blight.en: cause something to suffer, fail, or be spoiled

ex.en: Scandal blighted the final years of his career.
ex.ko: 스캔들이 그의 경력 말년을 망쳐 놓았다.

blitz.en: a sudden, intensive military attack, especially from the air

ex.en: The city suffered heavy damage during the blitz.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 대공습 동안 큰 피해를 입었다.

ex.en: Enemy planes blitzed the city at dawn.
ex.ko: 적군 비행기가 새벽에 도시를 급습했다.

blitz.en: a sudden, intense effort to achieve something quickly, especially in business or advertising

ex.en: The team launched an advertising blitz before the launch.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 출시 전에 대대적인 광고 총력전을 펼쳤다.

blubber.en: cry noisily, especially in a way that seems childish or excessive

ex.en: The child blubbered after dropping his ice cream.
ex.ko: 아이는 아이스크림을 떨어뜨리고 엉엉 울었다.

blubber.en: the thick layer of fat under the skin of whales and other large sea mammals

ex.en: Whale blubber helps them stay warm in cold water.
ex.ko: 고래의 지방층은 그들이 차가운 물에서 체온을 유지하는 데 도움을 준다.

blubbery.en: covered with a thick layer of fat; excessively fat

ex.en: The seal's blubbery body helps it survive freezing waters.
ex.ko: 물개의 두꺼운 지방질 몸은 얼어붙는 물속에서 살아남는 데 도움이 된다.

blurb.cue: 책 표지에 실리는 짧은 홍보 문구
blurb.en: a short description of a book, film, or product, written to promote it

ex.en: The blurb on the back cover praised the novel as a masterpiece.
ex.ko: 뒤표지의 홍보 문구는 그 소설을 걸작이라 칭찬했다.

ex.en: He was arrested for using a bogus passport.
ex.ko: 그는 위조 여권을 사용해 체포되었다.

bona fide.en: real, genuine, and not fake or fraudulent

ex.en: You must show a bona fide student ID to get the discount.
ex.ko: 할인을 받으려면 진짜 학생증을 제시해야 한다.

bout.en: a short period of illness or intense activity

ex.en: She suffered a bout of flu last week.
ex.ko: 그녀는 지난주 독감을 한바탕 앓았다.

bout.en: a contest or fight, especially in boxing or wrestling

ex.en: The champion won his title bout by knockout.
ex.ko: 챔피언은 타이틀전에서 녹아웃으로 승리했다.

brandish.en: wave or hold up something, especially a weapon, in a threatening or excited way

ex.en: The robber brandished a knife and demanded cash.
ex.ko: 강도는 칼을 휘두르며 현금을 요구했다.

breadwinner.en: the person in a family who earns the money needed to support the household

ex.en: After her husband's illness, she became the family's breadwinner.
ex.ko: 남편이 병을 앓은 후 그녀가 가족의 생계 부양자가 되었다.

brevity.en: the quality of expressing something in few words; shortness

ex.en: The editor praised the brevity of his writing.
ex.ko: 편집자는 그의 글의 간결함을 칭찬했다.

brim.en: be completely full, almost to the point of overflowing

ex.en: Her eyes brimmed with tears.
ex.ko: 그녀의 눈에 눈물이 가득 고였다.

brim.en: the upper edge of a container, or the wide bottom part of a hat

ex.en: He filled the glass to the brim.
ex.ko: 그는 잔을 가득 채웠다.

brimstone.en: an old-fashioned word for sulfur, often used in religious or literary contexts to describe hell

ex.en: The preacher warned of fire and brimstone.
ex.ko: 그 설교자는 지옥불과 유황을 경고했다.

brink.cue: 어떤 일이 막 벌어지기 직전 상태
brink.en: the point just before something, especially something dangerous or important, happens

ex.en: The two countries were on the brink of war.
ex.ko: 두 나라는 전쟁 직전 상태였다.

brood.en: think anxiously or sadly about something for a long time

ex.en: He sat brooding over his failure all evening.
ex.ko: 그는 저녁 내내 자신의 실패를 곱씹으며 앉아 있었다.

brood.en: a family of young birds or animals born or hatched together

ex.en: The hen watched over her brood of chicks.
ex.ko: 암탉은 한 배의 병아리들을 지켜보았다.

brouhaha.en: a lot of noisy and excited talk or reaction to something, often more than it deserves

ex.en: The brouhaha over the celebrity's tweet died down after a few days.
ex.ko: 그 유명인의 트윗을 둘러싼 소동은 며칠 후 잠잠해졌다.

buccaneer.en: a pirate, especially one who raided ships in the Caribbean in past centuries

ex.en: The museum has a exhibit on famous Caribbean buccaneers.
ex.ko: 그 박물관에는 유명한 카리브해 해적들에 대한 전시가 있다.

buccaneer.en: a person who takes bold and often risky actions in business

ex.en: The startup was founded by a young business buccaneer.
ex.ko: 그 스타트업은 젊고 대담한 사업가에 의해 설립되었다.

buffoonery.en: silly or ridiculous behavior meant to amuse others

ex.en: The meeting descended into buffoonery when the projector broke.
ex.ko: 프로젝터가 고장 나자 회의는 익살스러운 소동으로 변했다.

ex.en: The city has a burgeoning tech industry.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 급성장하는 기술 산업을 가지고 있다.

buzzard.en: a large bird of prey that soars over open land

ex.en: A buzzard circled slowly above the field.
ex.ko: 말똥가리 한 마리가 들판 위를 천천히 맴돌았다.

caffeinate.en: put caffeine into a drink, or consume caffeine to feel more awake

ex.en: She caffeinated herself with three cups of coffee before the exam.
ex.ko: 그녀는 시험 전에 커피 세 잔으로 카페인을 섭취했다.

calamitous.en: causing great and often sudden damage or distress; disastrous

ex.en: The flood had a calamitous effect on the region's economy.
ex.ko: 그 홍수는 그 지역 경제에 재앙적인 영향을 미쳤다.

calamitously.en: in a way that causes great and often sudden damage or distress

ex.en: The plan went calamitously wrong.
ex.ko: 그 계획은 재앙적으로 잘못되었다.

calamity.en: an event that causes great and often sudden damage or suffering; a disaster

ex.en: The earthquake was the worst calamity in the country's history.
ex.ko: 그 지진은 그 나라 역사상 최악의 참사였다.

callous.en: showing no concern for the feelings or suffering of others

ex.en: It was a callous remark to make to someone grieving.
ex.ko: 슬퍼하는 사람에게 그런 무정한 말을 하다니.

callously.cue: 타인의 고통을 신경 쓰지 않고 무정하게
callously.en: in a way that shows no concern for the feelings or suffering of others

ex.en: The company callously ignored the workers' complaints.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 노동자들의 불만을 무정하게 무시했다.

calorific.en: producing heat or energy, especially relating to the calorie content of food

ex.en: Chocolate has a high calorific value.
ex.ko: 초콜릿은 칼로리 함량이 높다.

capacious.en: having a lot of space inside; roomy

ex.en: She packed everything into a capacious travel bag.
ex.ko: 그녀는 모든 것을 널찍한 여행 가방에 챙겨 넣었다.

carbonize.en: turn something into carbon, typically by burning it

ex.en: The wood was carbonized by the intense heat.
ex.ko: 그 나무는 강렬한 열에 의해 탄화되었다.

catastrophically.en: in a way that causes sudden, extreme, and disastrous harm

ex.en: The bridge failed catastrophically during the storm.
ex.ko: 그 다리는 폭풍 속에서 파국적으로 붕괴되었다.

cay.en: a small, low island made of sand or coral, typically found in tropical waters

ex.en: They sailed to a deserted cay off the coast of Florida.
ex.ko: 그들은 플로리다 해안 근처의 무인 산호초 섬으로 항해했다.

centralized.en: controlled by a single central authority or location, rather than spread out

ex.en: The company moved to a more centralized management system.
ex.ko: 그 회사는 더 중앙집권화된 관리 체계로 전환했다.

choreographical.en: relating to the arrangement or design of dance movements

ex.en: The show's choreographical style blended ballet and hip-hop.
ex.ko: 그 공연의 안무 스타일은 발레와 힙합을 혼합했다.

choreography.en: the sequence of steps and movements designed for a dance or performance

ex.en: She studied the choreography for weeks before the recital.
ex.ko: 그녀는 발표회 전 몇 주간 안무를 익혔다.

chronologically.en: in the order in which events happened

ex.en: The exhibits are arranged chronologically, from oldest to newest.
ex.ko: 전시품들은 가장 오래된 것부터 최신 것까지 연대순으로 배치되어 있다.

chronology.en: a record or arrangement of events in the order in which they happened

ex.en: The book provides a detailed chronology of the war.
ex.ko: 그 책은 그 전쟁에 대한 상세한 연대기를 제공한다.

chrysalis.en: the hard protective case in which a caterpillar changes into a butterfly or moth

ex.en: The caterpillar spent two weeks inside its chrysalis.
ex.ko: 그 애벌레는 번데기 속에서 2주를 보냈다.

cinematographer.en: a person whose job is to operate the camera and control the visual style of a film

ex.en: The cinematographer won an award for the film's stunning visuals.
ex.ko: 그 촬영감독은 영화의 놀라운 영상미로 상을 받았다.

cinematography.en: the art and technique of shooting and lighting scenes for a film

ex.en: The film was praised for its beautiful cinematography.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 아름다운 촬영 기법으로 호평받았다.

circumnavigate.en: travel all the way around something, especially the world, by sea or air

ex.en: The sailor circumnavigated the globe alone.
ex.ko: 그 뱃사람은 혼자 세계 일주를 했다.

circumnavigation.en: a journey all the way around something, especially the world, by sea or air

ex.en: Her circumnavigation of the world took eight months.
ex.ko: 그녀의 세계 일주는 8개월이 걸렸다.

clinch.en: finally settle or secure something, such as a deal or victory

ex.en: The team clinched the championship with a last-minute goal.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 막판 골로 우승을 확정지었다.

clinch.en: (in boxing) the act of holding onto an opponent to avoid being hit

ex.en: The referee separated the boxers from their clinch.
ex.ko: 심판은 클린치 상태의 두 복서를 떼어놓았다.

cloister.en: a place, such as a monastery, where monks or nuns live apart from the world

ex.en: The monks have lived in the cloister for decades.
ex.ko: 수도승들은 수십 년간 그 수도원에서 살아왔다.

cloister.cue: 수도원 안뜰을 둘러싼 지붕 달린 통로
cloister.en: a covered walkway surrounding a courtyard, typically in a monastery or college

ex.en: Students strolled through the cloister between classes.
ex.ko: 학생들은 수업 사이 회랑을 거닐었다.

cloistered.en: kept away from the outside world, especially in a quiet and protected way

ex.en: She grew up in a cloistered environment, rarely leaving the estate.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 사유지를 거의 벗어나지 않는 세상과 격리된 환경에서 자랐다.

coax.en: gently and persistently persuade someone to do something

ex.en: She coaxed her little brother into eating his vegetables.
ex.ko: 그녀는 남동생을 구슬려 채소를 먹게 했다.

cocoon.cue: 애벌레가 몸을 보호하려고 만드는 실 껍질
cocoon.en: a silky covering that a caterpillar or other insect larva spins around itself for protection

ex.en: The moth emerged from its cocoon after two weeks.
ex.ko: 그 나방은 2주 후 고치에서 나왔다.

cocoon.en: protect or isolate yourself from the outside world, as if wrapped in a protective covering

ex.en: He cocooned himself in his apartment during the storm.
ex.ko: 그는 폭풍이 부는 동안 아파트에 틀어박혔다.

ex.en: She made a cogent argument for the new policy.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 정책에 대해 설득력 있는 주장을 펼쳤다.

cogently.en: in a clear, logical, and convincing way

ex.en: He explained the theory cogently, even to beginners.
ex.ko: 그는 초보자에게도 그 이론을 설득력 있게 설명했다.

cognitive.en: relating to mental processes such as thinking, understanding, and remembering

ex.en: The puzzle is designed to improve cognitive skills.
ex.ko: 그 퍼즐은 인지 능력을 향상시키도록 고안되었다.

collide.en: crash violently into something while moving

ex.en: The two cars collided at the intersection.
ex.ko: 두 차가 교차로에서 충돌했다.

collide.en: be in conflict or disagree strongly with something

ex.en: Her views on the project collided with her boss's.
ex.ko: 그 프로젝트에 대한 그녀의 견해는 상사의 견해와 상충했다.

collider.en: a scientific machine that accelerates particles and forces them to crash into each other for research

ex.en: Scientists used the collider to study subatomic particles.
ex.ko: 과학자들은 그 입자 가속기를 이용해 아원자 입자를 연구했다.

colloquial.en: used in informal, everyday conversation rather than formal writing or speech

ex.en: "Gonna" is a colloquial form of "going to."
ex.ko: 'gonna'는 'going to'의 구어체 표현이다.

colloquially.en: in an informal way, as used in everyday conversation

ex.en: The disease is colloquially known as "the flu."
ex.ko: 그 질병은 일상적으로 '독감'이라고 불린다.

colloquium.en: an academic meeting or seminar in which experts discuss a particular subject

ex.en: She presented her research at the department's weekly colloquium.
ex.ko: 그녀는 학과의 주간 콜로퀴엄에서 자신의 연구를 발표했다.

combustion.cue: 물질이 산소와 반응해 열과 빛을 내며
combustion.en: the process of burning something, in which a substance reacts with oxygen to produce heat and light

ex.en: The engine works through the combustion of fuel.
ex.ko: 그 엔진은 연료의 연소를 통해 작동한다.
